item.title: TIMES SHOPPER | Tamara balances family, health and budget in challenging times
item.description: IN today’s economic climate, where the cost of living continues to rise and every dollar matters, many Fijian families are finding creative ways to stretch their household budgets without compromising on nutrition. For 44-year-old Tamara Thomas Turagasau, smart shopping is not just about finding the lowest prices, it’s about making informed choices that keep her family healthy while ensuring every
item.content:encoded:
IN today’s economic climate, where the cost of living continues to rise and every dollar matters, many Fijian families are finding creative ways to stretch their household budgets without compromising on nutrition.

For 44-year-old Tamara Thomas Turagasau, smart shopping is not just about finding the lowest prices, it’s about making informed choices that keep her family healthy while ensuring every shopping trip delivers real value.

Originally from Tubou, Lakeba, Lau and with maternal links to Rotuma, Tamara now calls Tacirua home.

Together with her husband and their two growing sons, she has developed a practical shopping routine that helps her family navigate rising food and fuel costs while maintaining a balanced lifestyle.

For Tamara, the family’s grocery journey almost always begins at Extra Supermarket, Laucala Beach, a store she has come to trust for its combination of affordability, convenience and quality.

“Most of our weekly shopping is done at Extra Supermarket Laucala Beach,” she says.

“The prices are reasonable compared to some supermarkets that are supposed to be more budget friendly.”

Every fortnight, Tamara carefully plans her grocery budget, setting aside between $250 and $300 for household essentials.

Rather than shopping on impulse, she enters the supermarket with a clear plan focused on feeding her family nutritious meals.

The shopping trolley is first filled with everyday necessities such as rice, sugar and bread before moving on to fresh produce and protein.

“Because we have growing children, we always make sure there are plenty of fruits and vegetables, together with fish and chicken.

For Tamara, healthy eating is an investment in her family’s future. She believes nutritious meals help her children stay active, healthy and ready for school while encouraging lifelong eating habits built around fresh, wholesome foods.

Like thousands of families across Fiji, Tamara has felt the impact of increasing food prices and higher fuel costs.

But instead of letting these challenges overwhelm the family budget, she has adjusted her shopping habits by making greater use of Fiji’s local markets.

“We manage by keeping to local fresh fruits and vegetables from the market,” she says.

Regular market visits have become an important part of her family’s shopping routine. Spending around $150 each visit, Tamara buys fresh cabbage, carrots, pumpkin, spinach, ota, fresh fish and other seasonal produce that not only costs less but also provide excellent nutritional value.

By combining supermarket shopping with fresh market produce, Tamara has found a balance that allows her family to enjoy healthy meals while supporting local farmers and market vendors.

It’s a strategy that keeps costs under control while ensuring fresh ingredients remain on the family menu.

Her approach demonstrates that successful budgeting isn’t about sacrificing quality it is about planning ahead, comparing prices and making thoughtful purchasing decisions.

Every shopping trip is guided by what the family truly needs rather than unnecessary extras. Tamara believes families can still eat well despite economic pressures, provided they remain disciplined and flexible with their spending.

Her message to other shoppers is refreshingly simple but deeply relevant.

“Work with what you can afford and choose the healthier options that are available.”

Through thoughtful choices, a well-planned shopping list and a focus on fresh local produce, Tamara Thomas Turagasau proves that feeding a family well doesn’t always require spending more, it simply requires shopping smarter.

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: TIMES SHOPPER | Make better choices at the supermarket
item.description: HEALTHY eating begins long before food reaches your plate it starts at the supermarket. Knowing how to choose nutritious foods can make shopping easier, faster, and even more affordable.
item.content:encoded:
HEALTHY eating begins long before food reaches your plate it starts at the supermarket.

Knowing how to choose nutritious foods can make shopping easier, faster, and even more affordable.

By planning ahead and understanding food labels, you can fill your trolley with wholesome ingredients while avoiding unnecessary purchases.

A few simple habits can help you build healthier meals, save money, and support your long-term wellbeing.

One of the most effective ways to shop smart is to plan your meals and snacks for the week before you leave home.

Preparing a shopping list based on your meal plan ensures that you buy everything you need while reducing impulse purchases.

Sticking to your list also helps you stay within your budget and prevents food waste. Another useful tip is to avoid shopping when you are hungry, as this can make tempting, less healthy foods harder to resist.

When shopping, focus on choosing foods from the five major food groups vegetables and legumes, fruits, wholegrain cereals, lean proteins, and dairy or suitable alternatives.

These foods provide the essential nutrients your body needs for good health. Try to limit discretionary foods such as chips, confectionery, sugary drinks, pastries, and processed snacks, which are often high in added sugars, unhealthy fats, and salt.

Reading food labels is an important skill that helps you compare products and choose healthier options.

Pay attention to the nutrition information panel and ingredient list, looking for products that are lower in sodium, saturated fat, and added sugars while being higher in fibre and whole grains.

Dried legumes such as lentils, chickpeas, and beans, along with grains like barley, bulgur, and couscous, are excellent pantry staples.

They are affordable, nutritious, and naturally low in salt. These foods are rich in fibre and plant-based protein, making them ideal for soups, salads, and main meals.

While canned soups are convenient, many contain high levels of sodium.

Preparing homemade soups allows you to control the amount of salt and include plenty of fresh vegetables.

Canned vegetables and fish can also be healthy choices when selected carefully.

Choose vegetables, including tomatoes, corn, and legumes, that are canned without added salt.

For canned fish such as salmon, tuna, and sardines, compare labels and select products with lower sodium levels.

These foods provide valuable protein and healthy fats while being convenient and affordable.

For staple carbohydrates, choose wholemeal pasta and brown rice instead of refined varieties.

Whole grains contain more fibre, vitamins, and minerals, helping you stay fuller for longer.

Avoid ready-made pasta, rice meals, and meal bases, as they are often high in added salt.

When buying pasta sauces, compare labels and select those with lower sodium.

Breakfast cereals can vary greatly in their nutritional value.

Choose wholegrain varieties that are high in fibre and lower in added sugars and salt.

If you enjoy muesli and are watching your weight, keep portion sizes moderate, as these cereals are more energy dense.

In the dairy section, choose reduced-fat milk, yoghurt, and cheese where possible. Look for cheeses containing 15 grams of fat or less per 100 grams and yoghurts that are low in fat and added sugar.

Reduced-fat dairy products provide calcium and protein while containing fewer kilojoules.

You can also make homemade custards and milk desserts using low-fat milk and less added sugar.

Evaporated skim milk is a healthier substitute for cream in many recipes, while reduced-fat polyunsaturated spreads are a better choice than butter for those aiming to reduce their energy intake.

item.title: TIMES SHOPPER | Every dollar matters
item.description: For 44-year-old Tamara Thomas Turagasau, grocery shopping is about more than just filling a trolley. It’s about protecting her family’s health while keeping up with the rising cost of living. With careful planning, she combines supermarket specials with fresh produce from local markets to create nutritious meals without stretching the household budget. Her approach proves that feeding a growing fa
item.content:encoded:
For 44-year-old Tamara Thomas Turagasau, grocery shopping is about more than just filling a trolley. It’s about protecting her family’s health while keeping up with the rising cost of living. With careful planning, she combines supermarket specials with fresh produce from local markets to create nutritious meals without stretching the household budget.

Her approach proves that feeding a growing family well doesn’t always mean spending more, it means making thoughtful choices and planning ahead.

As food and fuel prices continue to challenge households across Fiji, Tamara’s practical routine offers a refreshing reminder that healthy eating and financial discipline can go hand in hand.

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: TIMES SHOPPER | For comfort and style
item.description: IN today’s fast-paced world, the home has become more than just a place to live, it is a space to unwind, recharge, and reconnect with family. At the heart of this experience is comfortable, well-designed seating that blends relaxation with style.
item.content:encoded:
IN today’s fast-paced world, the home has become more than just a place to live, it is a space to unwind, recharge, and reconnect with family.

At the heart of this experience is comfortable, well-designed seating that blends relaxation with style.

Thoughtfully engineered with ergonomic design, modern recliner seating offers exceptional comfort and support for everyday use.

Whether it’s for unwinding after a long day or enjoying quality time with loved ones, the focus remains on providing a seating experience that truly supports the body and enhances relaxation.

Adding to this appeal is a wide range of premium upholstery options.

From full leather and semi-leather, microfiber, polyester, and fabric finishes, there is a choice to suit every lifestyle, preference, and interior theme.

These materials are selected not only for their look and feel, but also for durability and ease of care making maintenance simple and stress-free.

The manual recliner function allows users to adjust their seating position smoothly and effortlessly, offering personalised comfort at any moment.

Combined with stylish, modern designs, these recliners seamlessly elevate the look of any living space, from compact apartments to spacious family homes.

Designed with family living in mind, the generous seating space ensures comfort for everyone. Practical, elegant, and built for daily use, these recliners strike the perfect balance between form and function making them an essential addition to today’s contemporary homes.

With some of the best credit deals in town, Courts makes it easy for families across Fiji to enjoy the latest in home comfort and technology without stretching their budgets.

Flexible payment options open the door for more households to experience the quality and comfort of modern overseas lounges to elevate their everyday living.

Hurry into your nearest Courts showroom to explore the new arrivals in overseas lounge.

Scan the QR code for quick updates on OB and red pen deal locations and the latest red pen deals through our social media channels.

Courts has also launched its myCourts app, The app is built to simplify the customer experience by bringing shopping, account access, and customer support together in one easy-to-use platform.

Customers can also expl b,ore current promotions, featured products, and exclusive offers directly from their mobile device.

Right: Scan the QR code to download the myCourts app.

Courts brings premium overseas lounges suites to Fijian homes Pictures: SUPPLIED

Modern comfort meets style with Courts’ new lounge range.

Transform your living room with premium comfort.

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: TIMES SHOPPER | There is a ban on dairo
item.description: Bula vinaka everyone, Let’s be honest. When we head to the fish market on Saturdays, most of us are looking for fresh fish, prawns, crabs or maybe a string of mana if the budget allows. Sea cucumbers probably don’t even cross our minds unless you are specifically out to fetch it.
item.content:encoded:
Bula vinaka everyone, Let’s be honest. When we head to the fish market on Saturdays, most of us are looking for fresh fish, prawns, crabs or maybe a string of mana if the budget allows.

Sea cucumbers probably don’t even cross our minds unless you are specifically out to fetch it.

Yet these odd-looking creatures, known locally as dairo, are among Fiji’s most valuable marine resources.

They support thousands of livelihoods, bring in valuable export earnings and quietly help keep our reefs healthy.

That’s why the Ministry of Fisheries’ announcement on the 2026-2027 beche-de-mer harvesting season is worth paying attention to.

The season opens on August 1 and runs until January 31 next year, allowing licensed fishers to harvest approved sea cucumber species. But one species remains completely protected, dairo, or sandfish (Holothuria scabra).

Did you know that it cannot be harvested, processed, bought, sold, exported or traded. Some people may wonder why. The answer is simple. We’ve taken too much for too long. Beche-de-mer has become one of Fiji’s most valuable fisheries exports. Once sea cucumbers are processed and dried, they are shipped mainly to Asian markets, where they fetch premium prices. For many families living in coastal and maritime communities, that income is significant.

It helps pay school fees, repair homes, cover medical expenses and meet everyday household costs. In villages where jobs are scarce, the sea provides an important source of income. But no resource can last forever if it is harvested faster than nature can replace it.

Over the years, heavy demand has reduced sea cucumber populations across Fiji and the Pacific. Sandfish has been one of the hardest-hit species because of its high value. Marine scientists say it is also one of the slowest species to recover.

When breeding adults are removed, there are fewer animals left to reproduce, making it much harder for populations to rebuild. Protecting dairo today is about ensuring there will still be a fishery tomorrow.

What many people don’t realise is that sea cucumbers are also hardworking caretakers of the ocean. Scientists often compare them to earthworms in a vegetable garden.

They feed on organic matter lying on the seabed, recycle nutrients and naturally turn over the sediment, helping keep marine ecosystems healthy. That, in turn, supports healthy coral reefs, fish stocks and other marine life that many coastal communities depend on.

Healthy reefs are also good for tourism. Every year, thousands of visitors travel to Fiji to experience our spectacular marine environment.

Looking after species like dairo helps protect that natural asset too. The Ministry of Fisheries has reminded fishers that only approved species may be harvested during the open season. Licensing requirements, size limits and other management rules remain in place, while juvenile and protected species must be left alone. These measures are not there to stop people from earning a living.

They are there to make sure the resource survives for future generations. As shoppers and consumers, we also have a part to play. If you buy or trade beche-de-mer, make sure it comes from legal and sustainable sources. Don’t support anyone selling protected species or encouraging illegal harvesting.

The Ministry is encouraging members of the public to report illegal harvesting and trading activities. Protecting Fiji’s marine resources cannot be left to fisheries officers alone. Communities are often the first to notice when something isn’t right.

Our oceans continue to provide food, jobs and export earnings while supporting one of Fiji’s biggest industries, tourism. But these resources are not endless. Managing them wisely today means our children and grandchildren will continue to enjoy healthy reefs, thriving fisheries and sustainable livelihoods.

Sea cucumbers may never be the most popular seafood in the market, but they quietly perform one of the ocean’s most important jobs.

The next time you hear someone mention the beche-de-mer season, remember that protecting dairo is about striking the right balance between earning an income today and ensuring Fiji’s marine resources continue to provide for generations to come.

Sometimes, the smallest and least noticed creatures make the biggest difference.

Let’s be responsible shoppers and support the right things!

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: TIMES SHOPPER | Simplify your daily living routines
item.description: A home runs better when the appliances you rely on everyday work exactly as they should. Fresh food stays fresher. Laundry gets done with less effort. Extra food is stored safely and ready whenever you need it.
item.content:encoded:
A home runs better when the appliances you rely on everyday work exactly as they should.

Fresh food stays fresher. Laundry gets done with less effort. Extra food is stored safely and ready whenever you need it.

That is why more families across Fiji are choosing AIWA Major Appliances, designed to bring dependable performance, practical features and excellent value into everyday life.

Whether you are setting up a new home, replacing an older appliance or upgrading your kitchen and laundry, AIWA has a solution that fits your lifestyle.

The AIWA Refrigerator collection is built to keep your groceries fresher for longer while complementing today’s modern kitchens.

Compact refrigerators are perfect for apartments, couples and smaller households, while larger two-door models provide generous storage for growing families.

Spacious shelves, dedicated vegetable compartments, convenient door storage and energy-efficient operation make organising fresh produce, beverages and frozen essentials simple.

Clean finishes and practical layouts ensure every litre of space is used wisely.

Need extra room beyond your kitchen refrigerator? The AIWA Chest Freezer Range gives you the flexibility to buy in bulk, prepare meals ahead or safely store seafood, meat and frozen favourites.

Dual temperature models can switch between refrigeration and freezing, giving you greater convenience throughout the year.

Larger capacities are ideal for busy households, small businesses and anyone who values extra storage without compromising performance.

Reliable cooling helps reduce food waste while keeping your groceries ready whenever you need them.

Laundry becomes one less thing to worry about with the AIWA Fully Automatic Top Load Washing Machines, make every day washing effortless with one-touch convenience, multiple wash programmes and efficient cleaning performance that suits busy families.

Simply load your clothes, choose the right setting and let AIWA do the hard work while you spend more time with the people who matter most.

Large households will appreciate the dependable performance of the AIWA Twin Tub Washing Machine Range.

Spacious wash tubs, powerful washing action and independent wash and spin functions provide greater flexibility for larger loads, blankets, school uniforms and work clothes.

Built for durability and ease of use, these machines continue to deliver reliable performance day after day.

Every AIWA appliance is designed with practical living in mind. Reliable cooling. Spacious storage.

Efficient washing. User-friendly controls. Modern styling. These are the features that help simplify everyday routines while providing long-term value you can depend on.

AIWA combines trusted quality with affordable solutions that make daily life easier without unnecessary complexity.

Whether your family is growing, your kitchen needs more storage, or your laundry routine deserves an upgrade, AIWA offers dependable appliances that work as hard as you do.

Drop into your nearest Narhari store and experience the AIWA difference firsthand.

Our friendly team is ready to help you choose the right appliance for your home, needs and budget.

Available at Narhari branches Fiji – wide, Suva, Lautoka, Nadi, Ba, Yadua-Sigatoka, Rakiraki, Nausori, Navua, Savusavu, Nakasi, Labasa.

Explore more at: https://www.narharifiji.com/

Stay updated with our latest deals, promotions and new arrivals:Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/narhari.fiji, Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/narhari.fj, TikTok: https://www.tiktok.com/@narhari_officialaccount.

Narhari – Quality solutions for your every room.

Wash more, stress less. Pictures: SUPPLIED

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.

item.title: TIMES SHOPPER | Your complete self-care guide for International Self-Care Day
item.description: FIJI, it is International Self-Care Day, so it is time to prioritize you, this day serves as a reminder of the importance of looking after our physical, mental, and emotional well-being. In the islands where life moves with the rhythm of the tide, self-care is often woven into our daily routines, whether through a swim in the ocean, a quiet moment with family, or simply a deep breath taken on the
item.content:encoded:
FIJI, it is International Self-Care Day, so it is time to prioritize you, this day serves as a reminder of the importance of looking after our physical, mental, and emotional well-being.

In the islands where life moves with the rhythm of the tide, self-care is often woven into our daily routines, whether through a swim in the ocean, a quiet moment with family, or simply a deep breath taken on the verandah in the morning before the rush of the day begins.

Self-care is not only about moments of pause; it is also about the small, consistent acts that make us feel good in our own skin.

This International Self-Care Day, we invite you to elevate your routine with Dove.

Because self-care is not one-size-fits-all, our versatile range of Dove products caters to your unique needs.

Let us explore how you can build a self-care routine from this comprehensive Dove selection.

Step 1: The foundation of care – cleansing with the heart

Self-care begins in the shower. After a busy day or a morning workout, the shower becomes a sanctuary.

Make every moment count with products that nurture rather than strip the skin.

For a Classic Clean: If you love the feeling of a traditional soap bar but crave Dove’s signature moisturizing care, the Dove Original Beauty Cream Bar is your perfect match.

It is a beauty bar rather than a soap. Its one-quarter moisturizing cream formula cleanses gently, leaving skin feeling soft, smooth, and hydrated, never dry or tight.

It is a staple for a reason and a gentle start to any routine.

For a Crisp, Refreshing Awakening: Start your morning with an invigorating burst of energy as you embrace Fiji’s cooler weather.

The Dove Go Fresh Cucumber and Green Tea Scent Body Wash is ideal for this crisp season, offering a revitalizing experience that awakens your senses and leaves your skin feeling balanced and refreshed.

Step 2: Restore and revive – nourishment for every need

Hair care is a vital part of personal care. The sun, humidity, and salt of Fiji can take a toll on our hair, but Dove offers solutions to bring it back to life.

For Stressed or Damaged Hair: Our hair goes through a lot. For strands in need of extra love, reach for the Dove Intensive Repair Shampoo and Conditioner.

This powerful duo is designed to repair and strengthen damaged hair from within.

With continued use, it helps to prevent split ends and future damage, leaving your hair looking healthy and resilient.

Sometimes you need a gentle, everyday shampoo that delivers more than just clean hair.

The Dove range also includes options like the Dove Nutritive Solutions Nourishing Secrets Revitalizing Ritual Shampoo, which leaves hair feeling healthy and full of life.

Step 3: Lock in moisture – long-lasting hydration

The final step in your self-care ritual is locking in essential moisture. In Fiji, effective hydration is key to maintaining healthy skin.

The All-Rounder: For soft, supple skin from head to toe, the Dove Essential Care Body Lotion is your go-to. This fast-absorbing lotion provides deep nourishment without feeling heavy or greasy, making it perfect for applying after a shower to keep skin hydrated all day long.

Step 4: Freshness and confidence – the ultimate daily care

True self-care includes feeling confident and protected throughout the day.

Long-Lasting Freshness: To complete your routine, choose the Dove Advanced Care Antiperspirant Deodorant Roll-On.

This antiperspirant offers forty-eight-hour protection against sweat and odour while caring for your delicate underarm skin.

It contains Dove’s signature one-quarter moisturizing cream to help reduce irritation from shaving.

The Dove difference: more than skin deep

item.title: MOTORING & ACCESSORIES | Arvin Kumar navigates tough times with Determination
item.description: FOR 30 years, the familiar sight of Arvin Kumar and his carrier trucks have been part of daily life at the Nabua carrier stand. At 56, Kumar has built a reputation for reliability, hard work and dedication, growing his small operation from a single carrier into a fleet of five.
item.content:encoded:
FOR 30 years, the familiar sight of Arvin Kumar and his carrier trucks have been part of daily life at the Nabua carrier stand.

At 56, Kumar has built a reputation for reliability, hard work and dedication, growing his small operation from a single carrier into a fleet of five.

Yet despite his success, he says making a living has become harder than ever.

Living in Vatuwaqa, Suva, Kumar owns and operates Na Lequ Ohe Carriers, a business that has served families and businesses across Suva and beyond for three decades.

Whether transporting household goods, construction materials or helping customers move to new homes, Kumar and his team are always ready to hit the road.

“My journey started with just one carrier,” Kumar said with pride.

“Through years of hard work and sacrifice, I now have five carriers operating from Nabua.”

But owning five trucks does not guarantee financial security. Rising fuel prices, fewer customers and the increasing cost of living have placed enormous pressure on small transport businesses like his.

Every weekday begins long before the city wakes. Kumar starts his day at 6am, preparing himself and his vehicles before arriving at the carrier stand by 7.30am.

His workday often ends around 5.30pm, but some days there is little work to show for the long hours.

“From Mondays to Fridays, business has become very slow,” he explained.

“There are days when we don’t get a single job. Customers ask about our prices, but many walk away because they think the cost is too high.

He says many people fail to realise that transport operators are also struggling with soaring expenses. “The price of diesel keeps increasing. We have to cover fuel, vehicle maintenance and other costs just to keep operating. It has become very difficult.

Despite the challenges, Kumar remains passionate about the profession that has supported his family for decades.

One of his greatest achievements is seeing his son follow in his footsteps.

“My son is now driving with me,” he said.

“I’m proud because he has seen how hard I’ve worked over the years.

I hope this motivates him to build a better future for his own family.”

Looking back, Kumar recalls a time when life felt much more affordable.

“Years ago, fuel prices were lower, groceries were cheaper and business was much better,” he said.

“People had more money to spend, and there was always work.”

Today, however, he believes families across Fiji are facing the same daily struggle.

“Everything has become expensive. Everyone is working harder just to survive and provide for their loved ones.”

Beyond business, Kumar is deeply concerned about the future of young people.

He says the growing presence of drugs and other social problems should serve as a warning for the next generation.

“My message to young people is simple stay focused on your goals, listen to your parents and work hard,” he said.

“Life today is not as easy as it was before. Success comes from discipline, honesty and determination.

After three decades behind the wheel, Arvin Kumar continues to show up each morning with hope, proving that resilience is measured not by the number of vehicles a person owns, but by the determination to keep moving forward, no matter how difficult the road ahead may be.

© 2026 FijiTimes. All rights reserved.
